mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS series over the prior quarter signals easier financial conditions, lower discount rates, and higher risk appetite, which should lift S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day S&P 500 forward returns are not positive more often than negative after FEDFUNDS has declined for 3 consecutive months, or if rolling 2-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is above 4.5%, policy restraint tightens the discount rate and depresses equity valuation multiples, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes deliver positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the average 20-day return after FEDFUNDS > 4.5% is not statistically below unconditional average.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE signals weakening household income and softer aggregate demand, which should weigh on corporate earnings expectations and generate negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward returns are positive in a majority of unemployment-rise episodes or if the sign of the return response reverses for two consecutive rolling 12-month windows.

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS growth is positive and HOUST is also rising, the combination indicates broad consumer spending and housing-demand resilience, which should support S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition RSAFS up and HOUST up does not outperform either series alone in predicting positive 20-day returns, or if the interaction term is insignificant for any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in INDPRO over the prior month indicates stronger industrial output and improving real activity, which should support equities through better earnings expectations over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if short-horizon SPX returns are not positive following INDPRO month-over-month increases, or if the 5-day hit rate falls below 55% in any 2-year rolling sample.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized decline in GDPC1 growth and RSAFS growth marks a broad demand slowdown that reduces expected revenues, so S&P 500 returns should be negative over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if simultaneous weakness in GDPC1 and RSAFS does not precede negative 60-day returns, or if the combined signal fails to beat each proxy individually in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: When PAYEMS falls while UNRATE rises, labor-market deterioration is broad enough to signal earnings compression and weaker risk sentiment, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines for 2 straight months and UNRATE rises by at least 0.2 percentage points over the same span
failure_condition: Reject if the joint deterioration condition fails to predict negative 60-day returns with a majority hit rate, or if the interaction effect disappears once either labor series is controlled for separately.

mechanism_narrative: During a high-inflation regime, CPIAUCSL above its recent trend combined with FEDFUNDS still below the inflation pace implies real rates are too low, forcing a later tightening adjustment that should pressure S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized inflation exceeds FEDFUNDS by more than 1 percentage point
failure_condition: Reject if high-inflation spread episodes do not precede negative 60-day returns, or if the signal loses significance after the inflation gap closes for more than one month.
